_note: Sprint D.4 example — UK domestic bathroom (2.7 m × 2.1 m) with a single BS EN 61558-2-5 shaver supply. The room is recorded as room_type = 'bathroom' (Part-7 enum exact-match) specifically to fire INV-12's special-locations cascade. The companion adjacent hall room provides a small ring final circuit so the IR is not single-circuit-only. PASS case: zero 230 V sockets are placed inside any Zone 0/1/2, so all 4 INV-12 sub-checks resolve to PASS.
site_brief: UK domestic bathroom + adjacent hall. The bathroom is a Part 7-701 location with bath (500-2200 × 0-700 mm footprint, 550 mm rim) and overhead shower head at 2100 mm AFFL. Only a shaver supply (BS EN 61558-2-5) is provided inside the bathroom; no general-purpose 230 V sockets are placed in any §701 zone. The bathroom shaver and the hall ring are both fed via 30 mA RCD per §701.411.3.3 cascaded constraint.
_cascade_disclosure: At v1.0 there is no dedicated cascade-small-power-uk-bathroom-pass example in special-locations (only the FAIL-case cascade-small-power-uk-bathroom-violation exists). This consumer example reuses the lighting-layout PASS cascade payload for the same UK bathroom geometry — the special-locations IR is fixture-anchor-agnostic (zones are derived from bath_1 + shower_1 anchors, not from luminaire vs socket layout), so the payload is engineering-equivalent for either downstream skill. A small-power-specific bathroom PASS cascade is deferred to the Wave 2 sprint that backfills cascade-small-power-uk-bathroom-pass on the special-locations side.
